A0.spanish: ¡Qué guay! (ej. ¡Esa película está qué guay!)
A0.english: How cool! (e.g., That movie is so cool!)

A0.spanish: Mola mucho (ej. Tu coche nuevo mola mucho)
A0.english: It's really cool / I like it a lot (e.g., Your new car is really cool)

A0.spanish: Tío / Tía (ej. ¡Hola tío! ¿Qué tal?)
A0.english: Dude / Man / Girl (e.g., Hey dude! How's it going?)

A0.spanish: Estar pelado (ej. No puedo salir hoy, estoy pelado)
A0.english: To be broke (e.g., I can't go out today, I'm broke)

A0.spanish: Currar (ej. Mañana me toca currar temprano)
A0.english: To work (e.g., Tomorrow I have to work early)

A0.spanish: Pasta (ej. Eso cuesta mucha pasta)
A0.english: Money / Dough (e.g., That costs a lot of money)

A0.spanish: Estar en la parra (ej. Oye, ¡despierta! Estás en la parra)
A0.english: To daydream / space out (e.g., Hey, wake up! You're spacing out)

A0.spanish: Chupado (ej. El examen estuvo chupado)
A0.english: Piece of cake / Super easy (e.g., The exam was a piece of cake)

A0.spanish: Pillar (ej. ¿Lo has pillado? / Vamos a pillar una cerveza)
A0.english: To get / catch / grab (e.g., Did you get it? / Let's grab a beer)

A0.spanish: ¡Ni de coña! (ej. ¿Ir allí a las 5 AM? ¡Ni de coña!)
A0.english: No way! / Not a chance! (e.g., Go there at 5 AM? No way!)

A0.spanish: Fliparlo (ej. ¡Vas a fliparlo con este juego!)
A0.english: To be blown away / amazed (e.g., You're going to be amazed by this game!)

A0.spanish: Buena onda (ej. Tu hermano es muy buena onda)
A0.english: Good vibes / Cool person (e.g., Your brother has really good vibes)

A0.spanish: Dar la lata (ej. Deja de dar la lata con eso)
A0.english: To bother / nag (e.g., Stop nagging about that)

A0.spanish: Me importa un pimiento (ej. Haz lo que quieras, me importa un pimiento)
A0.english: I couldn't care less (e.g., Do what you want, I couldn't care less)

A0.spanish: Estar como una cabra (ej. Ese chico está como una cabra)
A0.english: To be crazy / nuts (e.g., That guy is crazy)

A0.spanish: Tomar el pelo (ej. ¿Me estás tomando el pelo?)
A0.english: To pull someone's leg / kid (e.g., Are you pulling my leg?)

A0.spanish: Hacerse el sueco (ej. Le pregunté y se hizo el sueco)
A0.english: To play dumb (e.g., I asked him and he played dumb)

A0.spanish: Estar empapado (ej. Llovió mucho y estoy empapado)
A0.english: To be soaked / drenched (e.g., It rained hard and I'm drenched)

A0.spanish: Ser un rata (ej. ¡No seas rata y paga la cuenta!)
A0.english: To be cheap / stingy (e.g., Don't be cheap, pay the bill!)

A0.spanish: Ponerse las pilas (ej. Tenemos que ponernos las pilas con este proyecto)
A0.english: To get one's act together (e.g., We need to get our act together for this project)

A0.spanish: Estar hasta las narices (ej. Estoy hasta las narices de este tráfico)
A0.english: To be fed up (e.g., I'm fed up with this traffic)

A0.spanish: Irse por las ramas (ej. No te vayas por las ramas y responde)
A0.english: To beat around the bush (e.g., Don't beat around the bush and answer)

A0.spanish: Ir a su bola (ej. Él siempre va a su bola)
A0.english: To do one's own thing (e.g., He always does his own thing)

A0.spanish: Ser pan comido (ej. Aprender esto es pan comido)
A0.english: To be a piece of cake (e.g., Learning this is a piece of cake)

A0.spanish: Quedar con alguien (ej. Quedé con María a las seis)
A0.english: To meet up with someone (e.g., I'm meeting up with María at six)

A0.spanish: Estar sin blanca (ej. No tengo dinero, estoy sin blanca)
A0.english: To be penniless (e.g., I have no money, I'm penniless)

A0.spanish: Ser un bicho raro (ej. Ese tipo es un bicho raro)
A0.english: To be an oddball / weirdo (e.g., That guy is an oddball)

A0.spanish: Estar al caer (ej. El autobús está al caer)
A0.english: To be right around the corner / coming soon (e.g., The bus is right around the corner)

A0.spanish: Estar en su salsa (ej. En la fiesta ella estaba en su salsa)
A0.english: To be in one's element (e.g., At the party she was in her element)

A0.spanish: No tener abuela (ej. ¡Tú no tienes abuela! Te alabas solo)
A0.english: To show off / lack modesty (e.g., You really show off! You praise yourself)

A0.spanish: Tener mala leche (ej. Ten cuidado, ese perro tiene mala leche)
A0.english: To have a bad temper / bad intent (e.g., Careful, that dog has a bad temper)

A0.spanish: Montar un pollo (ej. Montó un pollo en la tienda)
A0.english: To make a scene (e.g., He made a scene in the store)

A0.spanish: Ser la caña (ej. ¡Ese concierto fue la caña!)
A0.english: To be awesome / the best (e.g., That concert was awesome!)

A0.spanish: Tener enchufe (ej. Consiguió el trabajo porque tiene enchufe)
A0.english: To have connections / pull strings (e.g., He got the job because he has connections)

A0.spanish: Ir pisando huevos (ej. ¡Acelera! Vas pisando huevos)
A0.english: To drive/walk super slow (e.g., Speed up! You're driving super slow)

A0.spanish: Meter la pata (ej. Lo siento, metí la pata)
A0.english: To mess up / put one's foot in it (e.g., I'm sorry, I messed up)

A0.spanish: Hacer la pelota (ej. No le hagas la pelota al jefe)
A0.english: To butter up / kiss up to someone (e.g., Don't kiss up to the boss)

A0.spanish: Darse prisa (ej. ¡Date prisa que llegamos tarde!)
A0.english: To hurry up (e.g., Hurry up, we're late!)

A0.spanish: Estar de mala gana (ej. Lo hizo de mala gana)
A0.english: To do something reluctantly (e.g., He did it reluctantly)

A0.spanish: Pasárselo pipa (ej. Nos lo pasamos pipa en la playa)
A0.english: To have a blast (e.g., We had a blast at the beach)

A0.spanish: Estar colado por alguien (ej. Está colado por su vecina)
A0.english: To have a crush on someone (e.g., He has a crush on his neighbor)

A0.spanish: Tener changüí (ej. Dame un poco de changüí)
A0.english: To give a break / leeway (e.g., Give me a little break)

A0.spanish: Ir de gorra (ej. Siempre come de gorra)
A0.english: To freeload (e.g., He always freeloads food)

A0.spanish: Cantar las 40 (ej. Le cantó las cuarenta por llegar tarde)
A0.english: To give someone a piece of one's mind (e.g., She gave him a piece of her mind for being late)

A0.spanish: Estar por las nubes (ej. Los precios están por las nubes)
A0.english: To be sky-high (e.g., Prices are sky-high)

A0.spanish: Tirar la casa por la ventana (ej. Para su boda tiraron la casa por la ventana)
A0.english: To spare no expense / splurge (e.g., For their wedding they splurged)

A0.spanish: Quedarse de piedra (ej. Me quedé de piedra cuando me contó la noticia)
A0.english: To be stunned / shocked (e.g., I was shocked when he told me the news)

A0.spanish: Poner la zancadilla (ej. Intentó ponerme la zancadilla en la empresa)
A0.english: To trip someone up / sabotage (e.g., He tried to sabotage me at the company)

A0.spanish: Ver los toros desde la barrera (ej. Es fácil opinar viendo los toros desde la barrera)
A0.english: To be a backseat driver / observe from afar (e.g., It's easy to judge observing from afar)

A0.spanish: No dar pie con bola (ej. Hoy no doy pie con bola en el trabajo)
A0.english: To not get anything right (e.g., Today I can't get anything right at work)
